title: hyRAD for birds
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"313iu","text":"This protocol builds on Suchan et al. (2016). It has been modified to work on fresh and museum samples of birds, specifically tested on Regent Honeyeater (Anthochaera phrygia).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":31,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":155,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9hvt","text":"The detailed protocol contains three main sections: (A) preparing ddRAD libraries as probes, (B) preparing whole genome libraries for samples of different quality, and (C) using probes to hybridise and isolate the same fragments from all samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bekvj","text":"Start with up to 600 ng whole genomic DNA per sample (our minimum was 14 ng), resuspended in 8 μl. Always include a negative control (containing no DNA, just buffer) in parallel to samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"db5vm","text":"All purification was done using Solid Phase Reversible Immobilization (SPRI) beads with different bead : sample ratios at each step (specified in the protocol). AMPure beads are commonly used. However, we used Seramag throughout the protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"csjmh","text":"All quantitation was done by fluorescence using Qubit (Invitrogen). Fragment Analyzer, Tapestation, or LabChip GXII can also be used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8s04m","text":"All visualization steps for size profiles were done using LabChip GXII (Caliper). Agarose gel, Fragment Analyzer, Tapestation, or Bioanalyzer can also be used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3afv0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"phdk","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"9il4k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"81uf2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cj3gt","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"1atc9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"D31FCD1687DA49F0A24A7AA6F6C75BED","height":null,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"hyRAD_01.jpg","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/59mizfw.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/59mizfw.jpg","width":null}},"1":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"68DE8321C44A4695976536CDBD5C4C79","height":null,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"hyRAD_02.jpg","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/59pizfw.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/59pizfw.jpg","width":null}}}}

Keep frozen until use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bjktu","text":"All sequences are given in 5'-->3' direction.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e2ebj","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"76htp","text":"AD_SphI adapter (IS7) - used in step #3","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cokun","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":4,"length":4},{"style":"bold","offset":12,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"8upnq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9sjau","text":"AD_MluCI adapter (IS8) - used in step #3","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8uen9","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":4,"length":4},{"style":"bold","offset":12,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"ft7m","text":"Pho = 5' Phosphorylation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9qpnk","text":"* = 3' phosphorothioate bond","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dcih","text":"These oligos are HPLC purified.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ajf0","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1cc4b","text":"To prepare 10 μM working solution, mix 10 μL of the RAD-P1.1 and RAD-P1.2 oligos (100 μM stock) with 80 μL of ultrapure water to obtain 100 μL of working solution.
